classificationSchemes.STI: UNSPSC (UN Standard Products and Services Code)
classificationSchemes.STI_hint: Broad multi-industry taxonomy maintained by the UN. Good general-purpose choice when no industry-specific scheme applies.
classificationSchemes.ECLASS_hint: Industrial and technical product classification, widely used in DACH manufacturing supply chains.
classificationSchemes.HS_hint: WCO customs nomenclature (HS codes). Required on cross-border goods invoices for customs clearance.
classificationSchemes.BDNS_hint: Specialist scheme used by the Belgian defence ministry. Unlikely outside that context.
classificationSchemes.CPV_hint: Required for EU public-sector procurement; identifies the kind of goods or services in tender contracts.
classificationSchemes.CV_hint: National customs tariff codes. Use when local customs rules require a country-specific scheme rather than HS.
classificationSchemes.GMN_hint: GS1 identifier for a product model (across variants) — broader than GTIN, which is per-SKU.
classificationSchemes.GS1_hint: Generic GS1 identifier (GLN, GTIN, SSCC, …) when none of the more specific codes here apply.
classificationSchemes.GTIN_hint: Per-SKU barcode (EAN/UPC). Identifies a specific item, not a category — use this if the line is a barcoded retail item.
classificationSchemes.ICC_hint: Industry-specific commodity coding. Rare in general commerce; check whether your buyer requires it.
classificationSchemes.MA_hint: The manufacturer's catalog number. Differs from VN (seller's catalog number) when the manufacturer and seller are different parties.
classificationSchemes.PV_hint: Identifies a campaign-specific variant of a base product. Used in retail promotion management.
classificationSchemes.SA_hint: An industry-agreed standard article number, sitting between manufacturer-private and globally unique schemes.
classificationSchemes.SRT_hint: Industry reporting code used in some statistical or regulatory contexts. Niche.
classificationSchemes.SRV_hint: Identifier for service offerings (rather than physical goods).
classificationSchemes.TSP_hint: References a pre-defined trade-service profile between two trading partners. Rarely used.
classificationSchemes.UA_hint: The end customer's own article number — used when the line passes through a reseller and the end buyer's catalog number is what matters.
classificationSchemes.UPC_hint: North American retail barcode. Mostly superseded by GTIN (which is the global superset).
classificationSchemes.VN_hint: The seller's own catalog number for this item. Distinct from MA (manufacturer's number) and UA (end-customer's number).
classificationSchemes.VP_hint: The seller's part number for a sub-component (vs VN, which is the catalog item).

icdSchemes.9918: SOCIETY FOR WORLDWIDE INTERBANK FINANCIAL, TELECOMMUNICATION S.W.I.F.T
icdSchemes.9942: Macedonia, the former Yugoslav Republic of VAT number
icdSchemes.9953: Holy See (Vatican City State) VAT number
icdSchemes.0002: System Information et Repertoire des Entreprise et des Etablissements: SIRENE
icdSchemes.0002_hint: 9-digit French legal-entity identifier (SIREN). Use SIRET (0009) when the establishment-level suffix matters.
icdSchemes.0009_hint: 14-digit French establishment identifier (SIREN + 5-digit suffix). Identifies a specific site, not just the legal entity.
icdSchemes.0060_hint: Dun & Bradstreet global business identifier. Frequently requested by US and large international buyers.
icdSchemes.0088_hint: GS1 location identifier; the standard pick in retail, logistics, and healthcare supply chains.
icdSchemes.0106_hint: Dutch chamber-of-commerce registration number — the standard B2B identifier in the Netherlands.
icdSchemes.0160_hint: GS1 product barcode (EAN/UPC superset). Use for retail items, not for parties or locations.
icdSchemes.0183_hint: Swiss enterprise identification number (UID/IDE). Mandatory on Swiss commercial invoices.
icdSchemes.0184_hint: Danish central business register number — the standard B2B identifier in Denmark.
icdSchemes.0188: Corporate Number of The Social Security and Tax Number System
icdSchemes.0190_hint: Dutch public-sector identifier. Required when invoicing Dutch government bodies.
icdSchemes.0192_hint: Norwegian central enterprise register number — the standard B2B identifier in Norway.
icdSchemes.0199_hint: 20-character ISO identifier for legal entities. Required by EU regulations for financial counterparties (banks, insurers, funds).
icdSchemes.0204_hint: Required by German federal and most state public-sector buyers — it routes the invoice to the correct receiving office.
icdSchemes.0208_hint: Belgian KBO/BCE enterprise number — the standard B2B identifier in Belgium.
icdSchemes.0210_hint: Italian fiscal code (16 alphanumeric for individuals, 11 digits for companies). Different from Partita IVA — use 0211 for the VAT number.
icdSchemes.0211_hint: 11-digit Italian VAT number. Use 0210 for the Codice Fiscale.
icdSchemes.0221: The registered number of the qualified invoice issuer
icdSchemes.0246_hint: Electronic business address for German B2B routing. Use 0204 (Leitweg-ID) for German public-sector buyers.
icdSchemes.0248: Oman Value Added Tax Identification Number (VATIN)
icdSchemes.9914_hint: Austrian VAT identification number (UID). Format starts with "ATU" followed by 8 digits.
icdSchemes.9930_hint: German VAT identification number (USt-IdNr.). Use 0204 (Leitweg-ID) instead when invoicing German public-sector buyers.

invoiceTypes.80: Debit note related to goods or services
invoiceTypes.84: Debit note related to financial adjustments
invoiceTypes.218: Final payment request based on completion of work
invoiceTypes.262: Consolidated credit note - goods and services
invoiceTypes.331: Commercial invoice which includes a packing list
invoiceTypes.420: Optical Character Reading (OCR) payment credit note
invoiceTypes.261_hint: A credit note issued by the buyer under a self-billing agreement.
invoiceTypes.325_hint: A non-binding draft for customs, prepayment or quotation purposes — not a VAT invoice and not booked as revenue.
invoiceTypes.326_hint: Bills part of an order or project before completion. The final invoice later settles the remainder.
invoiceTypes.380_hint: The standard outgoing invoice. The right pick for almost every B2B and B2G sale under EN 16931.
invoiceTypes.381_hint: Reduces or reverses an earlier invoice (return, discount, error fix). Amounts stay positive — the credit-note semantics handle the sign.
invoiceTypes.383_hint: Increases the amount the buyer owes, separately from the original invoice.
invoiceTypes.384_hint: A complete replacement that corrects a previously issued invoice; references the invoice it supersedes.
invoiceTypes.385_hint: One invoice covering several deliveries or earlier documents in a period.
invoiceTypes.386_hint: Invoices a payment due before delivery. Later netted off by the final invoice.
invoiceTypes.389_hint: The buyer issues the invoice on the seller's behalf, under a prior written agreement. Common for affiliate/commission payouts.
invoiceTypes.393_hint: An invoice whose receivable has been assigned to a factor; payment goes to the factor.
invoiceTypes.751_hint: Carries invoice data for booking only — not a payable invoice itself. Used in some accounting integrations.
invoiceTypes.875_hint: An interim invoice for a construction project (German Abschlagsrechnung).
invoiceTypes.877_hint: The closing invoice of a construction project, settling all prior partial invoices.

paymentMeans.30_hint: Generic bank transfer. Use 58 if you specifically want to signal a SEPA transfer.
paymentMeans.58_hint: SEPA-area euro transfer. The most common code on EU B2B invoices.
paymentMeans.59_hint: SEPA direct debit; requires a signed mandate from the buyer.
paymentMeans.49_hint: Non-SEPA direct debit. Use 59 for euro/SEPA mandates.
paymentMeans.48_hint: Generic payment card (when the card type isn't being distinguished).
paymentMeans.54_hint: Specifically a credit card (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, …).
paymentMeans.57_hint: Recurring transfer set up by the payer at their bank.
paymentMeans.42_hint: Legacy "transfer to bank account" code. Most invoicing software now uses 30 or 58 instead.
paymentMeans.68_hint: Web payment service or wallet (PayPal, Stripe Checkout, …). The free-text field usually names the provider.
paymentMeans.97_hint: Mutual offset of receivables and payables — no money actually moves.
paymentMeans.1_hint: Fallback when none of the other codes fits. Use sparingly — most software prefers a concrete code.

taxPointDateCode.3_hint: VAT becomes payable on the day the invoice is issued (the default in most jurisdictions).
taxPointDateCode.35_hint: VAT becomes payable when the goods or services are delivered. Common when delivery and invoicing diverge in time.
taxPointDateCode.432_hint: VAT becomes payable when payment falls due (cash-accounting schemes). Only valid in some countries — check local rules.

units.C62_hint: Dimensionless count — fits services, flat fees and abstract units of work (e.g. a lump-sum travel allowance).
units.EA_hint: Common in US and legacy systems. Use H87 for new records unless a receiver requires EA.
units.PCE_hint: Legacy alias of H87. Some bookkeeping systems still emit PCE; prefer H87 for new records.

vatCategory.S_hint: The normal VAT rate that applies in your country (e.g. 19 % in Germany).
vatCategory.Z_hint: Taxable at 0 %; the transaction is still in the VAT system. Different from "exempt" — input VAT can still be deducted.
vatCategory.E_hint: Outside the VAT system altogether (e.g. medical, education, some financial services). Input VAT is generally not deductible.
vatCategory.AE_hint: The buyer reports and pays the VAT, not you. Common for cross-border B2B services within the EU.
vatCategory.K_hint: EU B2B goods crossing a border; the buyer accounts for VAT in their country. Both parties need valid EU VAT IDs.
vatCategory.G_hint: Goods or services delivered outside the EU. Usually zero-rated; keep customs/export documentation.
vatCategory.O_hint: The transaction is not a VAT-relevant supply at all (e.g. salary recharges, dividends). Rare on commercial invoices.
vatCategory.L_hint: Spanish Canary Islands indirect tax (not VAT). Only use if your business is established there.
vatCategory.M_hint: Production/services tax of Spain's Ceuta and Melilla enclaves. Only use if your business is established there.
